name: Oppidum de Cluzel - Pech David – trail loop starting from the Pouvourville car park
name_original: Oppidum de Cluzel - Pech David – boucle trail au départ du parking de Pouvourville
region_intro: Jogging around Toulouse offers diverse landscapes, from the flat, shaded paths along the Canal du Midi and Garonne River banks to the more challenging, elevated trails of Pech David hill. The region is characterized by its extensive network of green spaces, including large parks and forests on the city's outskirts. These natural features provide varied terrain suitable for different running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Toulouse The most popular jogging route is Toulouse – trail loop along the Garonne, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) moderate trail that takes about 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This route offers classic European river views along the Garonne. Another top favourite among local runners is Oppidum de Cluzel - Pech David – trail loop starting from the Pouvourville car park, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This route provides elevation and panoramic views from Pech David hill. Local runners also love the Canal du Midi – Canal du Midi loop from Toulouse-Saint-Agne, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through the historic Canal du Midi, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Jogging around Toulouse is defined by the Garonne River banks, the UNESCO-listed Canal du Midi, and the varied terrain of Pech David hill and surrounding forests. This network offers options for different ability levels, from flat urban runs to more challenging trail experiences. The routes in Toulouse are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1300 reviews. More than 96000 runners have used komoot to explore Toulouse's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Toulouse?
A: Toulouse offers a vast network of running routes. You can explore over 2800 different trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Toulouse?
A: Toulouse provides a diverse range of running terrains. You'll find flat, shaded paths along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2665762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal du Midi Pathway&lt;/a&gt; and the Garonne River banks, perfect for long, tranquil runs. For those seeking more challenge, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172779/oppidum-de-cluzel-pech-david-trail-loop-starting-from-the-pouvourville-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pech David hill&lt;/a&gt; offers earthy trails with significant elevation and panoramic views. Additionally, the region boasts expansive green spaces like Parc de la Ramée and the Bouconne and Buzet Forests, providing varied natural surfaces.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Toulouse?
A: Yes, Toulouse has plenty of options for beginners or those looking for an easy run. There are over 300 easy routes available, many of which are flat and well-maintained, such as sections along the Canal du Midi or the Garonne River. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e978248029/trail-along-the-garonne-and-in-the-ramiers-park-blagnac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Garonne and in the Ramiers park – Blagnac&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.1 km loop.

Q: Are there challenging running trails with elevation around Toulouse?
A: For runners seeking a challenge, Toulouse offers routes with significant elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172779/oppidum-de-cluzel-pech-david-trail-loop-starting-from-the-pouvourville-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pech David hill&lt;/a&gt; is an ideal spot, featuring trails that climb to offer breathtaking panoramic views of the city. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10543077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pech David Recreation Area loop from Ramonville-Saint-Agne&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 275 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Toulouse?
A: The running routes in Toulouse are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1300 reviews. Runners frequently praise the scenic river views, the tranquil shaded paths of the Canal du Midi, and the varied terrain that caters to all fitness levels. Over 96,000 runners have used komoot to explore the region's diverse trails.

Q: Can I find shaded running paths for warmer days in Toulouse?
A: Absolutely. The banks of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40091015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal du Midi&lt;/a&gt; are famously lined with plane trees, providing extensive shade that is perfect for running during warmer periods. Additionally, the Bouconne and Buzet Forests on the outskirts of the city offer natural, shaded trails for a cooler running experience.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Toulouse?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Toulouse are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974176478/toulouse-trail-loop-along-the-garonne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toulouse – trail loop along the Garonne&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974177210/lake-balerme-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Balerme Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Toulouse?
A: Toulouse's running routes often pass by significant landmarks. Along the Garonne River, you can enjoy views of historical monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4780521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Neuf&lt;/a&gt; and La Grave. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35546019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal du Midi&lt;/a&gt; features charming structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3516895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castanet Lock&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brick Bridge at Pompertuzat&lt;/a&gt;. The city center also offers runs through the interconnected Jardin des Plantes, Grand Rond, and Jardin Royal.

Q: Are there running routes accessible by public transport in Toulouse?
A: Many running routes in Toulouse are easily accessible by public transport, especially those within the city center or along the Canal du Midi and Garonne River. Routes starting from areas like Toulouse-Saint-Agne or Jean Jaurès are typically well-connected, allowing you to reach the starting point conveniently.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly runs or walks in Toulouse?
A: Toulouse offers several family-friendly options for running or walking. Expansive green spaces like Parc de la Ramée and the interconnected Jardin des Plantes, Grand Rond, and Jardin Royal provide pleasant, generally flat paths suitable for families. These areas often have amenities and open spaces for children to enjoy.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Toulouse?
A: Many outdoor areas in and around Toulouse are dog-friendly, especially the natural trails in forests like Bouconne and Buzet, and along the Canal du Midi. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific parks or protected areas regarding leash requirements or restricted zones.

Q: Where can I find scenic river views while running in Toulouse?
A: For classic European river views, the extensive paths along both the east and west banks of the Garonne River are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974176478/toulouse-trail-loop-along-the-garonne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toulouse – trail loop along the Garonne&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful vistas of historical monuments and the river itself. You can also explore the Garonne's banks further at the Prairie des Filtres or near Blagnac.
tips:
The Pech-David hill offers a large park and offers magnificent views of Toulouse and the Pyrenees in the distance.
Traces of an ancient Gallic fortified town (oppidum) have been found there.
A cable car crossing the Garonne provides access from the west bank.
